Name: The Arc of South Florida – Project Thrive (early intervention childcare)
Autism / special‑needs language on site: Project Thrive serves infants and toddlers 6 weeks–5 years with “developmental and/or physical challenges,” described as an early intervention program focused on individualized goals and supports, functioning as a childcare/early learning setting rather than a clinic.

Name: Just Kids Centers – Preschool Special Needs Program
Autism / special‑needs language on site: States it is an “all‑inclusive” institution; explicitly lists “high functioning Autism” and ADHD as populations served, notes inclusive education model, and that they coordinate speech, occupational, and behavior therapy (including ABA) inside their preschool classrooms.

Name: Just Kids Centers – main preschool/academy
Autism / special‑needs language on site: The preschool/academy pages (linked from the main site) describe inclusive early childhood programs and reference the Special Needs Program that includes children with high‑functioning autism in general classrooms with supports.

Name: Inclusive preschool programs listed via Miami special‑needs daycare directory (therapeutic preschool model)
Autism / special‑needs language on site: This directory page includes several Miami programs described as “inclusive” preschool/daycare settings where children with developmental delays or autism learn alongside typically developing peers and have on‑site access to speech, occupational, and other therapies (e.g., programs specifically noting “classrooms designed for children on the Autism spectrum, including sensory…” and “inclusive education to children with developmental delays as well as typically developing children”).
